Electabuzz

#0124 Jynx #0126 Magmar
  • Contents
  • Info
  • Base stats
  • Evolution chart
  • Pokédex entries
  • Moves learned
  • Sprites
  • Locations
  • Language

Electabuzz is an Electric type Pokémon introduced in Generation 1.

Electabuzz

Additional artwork

Pokédex data

National № 0125
Type Electric
Species Electric Pokémon
Height 1.1 m (3′07″)
Weight 30.0 kg (66.1 lbs)
Abilities 1. Static
Vital Spirit (hidden ability)
Local № 0125 (Red/Blue/Yellow)
0155 (Gold/Silver/Crystal)
0125 (FireRed/LeafGreen)
0198 (Platinum)
0157 (HeartGold/SoulSilver)
0057 (Black 2/White 2)
0227 (Sun/Moon — Alola dex)
0296 (U.Sun/U.Moon — Alola dex)
0125 (Let's Go Pikachu/Let's Go Eevee)
0183 (Legends: Arceus)
0016 (The Crown Tundra)
0011 (The Indigo Disk)

Training

EV yield 2 Speed
Catch rate 45 (5.9% with PokéBall, full HP)
Base Friendship 50 (normal)
Base Exp. 172
Growth Rate Medium Fast

Breeding

Egg Groups Human-Like
Gender 75% male, 25% female
Egg cycles 25 (6,169–6,425 steps)

Base stats

HP 65
240 334
Attack 83
153 291
Defense 57
107 234
Sp. Atk 95
175 317
Sp. Def 85
157 295
Speed 105
193 339
Total 490 Min Max

The ranges shown on the right are for a level 100 Pokémon. Maximum values are based on a beneficial nature, 252 EVs, 31 IVs; minimum values are based on a hindering nature, 0 EVs, 0 IVs.

Evolution chart

#0239
Elekid
Electric
(Level 30)
#0125
Electabuzz
Electric
(trade holding Electirizer)
#0466
Electivire
Electric

Electabuzz changes

  • In Generation 1, Electabuzz has a base Special stat of 85.
  • In Generations 1-4, Electabuzz has a base experience yield of 156.
  • In Generations 2-7, Electabuzz has a base Friendship value of 70.

Pokédex entries

Red
Blue		 Normally found near power plants, they can wander away and cause major blackouts in cities.
Yellow If a major power outage occurs, it is certain that this POKéMON has eaten electricity at a power plant.
Gold Electricity runs across the surface of its body. In darkness, its entire body glows a whitish-blue.
Silver Its body constantly discharges electricity. Getting close to it will make your hair stand on end.
Crystal When two ELECTABUZZ touch, they control the electric currents to communicate their feelings.
Ruby
Sapphire		 When a storm arrives, gangs of this POKéMON compete with each other to scale heights that are likely to be stricken by lightning bolts. Some towns use ELECTABUZZ in place of lightning rods.
FireRed It loves to feed on strong electricity. It occasionally appears around large power plants and so on.
LeafGreen Normally found near power plants, they can wander away and cause major blackouts in cities.
Emerald When a storm approaches, it competes with others to scale heights that are likely to be stricken by lightning. Some towns use ELECTABUZZ in place of lightning rods.
Diamond
Pearl		 Half of all blackouts occur when this Pokémon appears at power plants and eats electricity.
Platinum It windmills its arms to slightly boost its punches. Foes have been known to escape in the meantime.
HeartGold Electricity runs across the surface of its body. In darkness, its entire body glows a whitish-blue.
SoulSilver Its body constantly discharges electricity. Getting close to it will make your hair stand on end.
Black
White		 It windmills its arms to slightly boost its punches. Foes have been known to escape in the meantime.
Black 2
White 2		 Research is progressing on storing lightning in Electabuzz so this energy can be used at any time.
X Electricity runs across the surface of its body. In darkness, its entire body glows a whitish blue.
Y It loves to feed on strong electricity. It occasionally appears around large power plants and so on.
Omega Ruby
Alpha Sapphire		 When a storm arrives, gangs of this Pokémon compete with each other to scale heights that are likely to be stricken by lightning bolts. Some towns use Electabuzz in place of lightning rods.
Sun Half of all sudden blackouts are caused by Electabuzz gathering at electric power plants and gobbling up electricity.
Moon Electricity leaks from it in amounts far greater than the amount of electricity it eats.
Ultra Sun Electricity permeates its body. It swings its arms round and round to charge up electricity before unleashing a punch.
Ultra Moon While it’s often blamed for power outages, the truth is the cause of outages is more often an error on the part of the electric company.
Let's Go Pikachu
Let's Go Eevee		 If a major power outage occurs, it is certain that this Pokémon has eaten electricity at a power plant.
Sword Many power plants keep Ground-type Pokémon around as a defense against Electabuzz that come seeking electricity.
Shield With the coming of a storm, many of these Pokémon will gather under tall trees and sit there waiting for lightning to strike.
Brilliant Diamond
Shining Pearl		 Half of all blackouts occur when this Pokémon appears at power plants and eats electricity.
Legends: Arceus Feeds on electrical energy. During sudden showers beneath looming thunderclouds, one can observe Electabuzz scaling tall trees, where the Pokémon will then wait for lightning to strike.

Legends: Z-A Scarlet/Violet

Moves learnt by level up

Electabuzz does not learn any level up moves in Pokémon Legends: Z-A.

Moves learnt by TM

Electabuzz cannot be taught any TM moves in Pokémon Legends: Z-A.

Moves learnt by level up

Electabuzz learns the following moves in Pokémon Scarlet & Violet at the levels specified.

Power
Acc.

Egg moves

Electabuzz does not learn any moves by breeding in Pokémon Scarlet & Violet.

Answers to Electabuzz questions

  • I am in india ,so I cannot trade my electabuzz, rhydon,magmar .so what is the solution ?
  • What do suggest, for defeating Red. Which electric type, Zapdos or Magneton. Any other pokemon please tell.
  • Electabuzz or Luxray? Attack , Special Atk and Speed?
  • Electivire and Dusclops Evolite question?
  • whats a good place to train an electabuzz for the pokemon league on platinum
  • Where i can found Electabuzz in Pokemon Silver?
  • Would Electabuzz work well with Choice Scarf and Electro Ball?
  • Magmar/elctabuzz migrate evolving?
  • Looking for Electabuzz in Pokémon Gold and there is no grass on Route 10?
  • Who should I switch out for Zapdos(If I should) in Pokemon Red?

  • View more questions on PokéBase »

Other languages

English Electabuzz
Japanese エレブー (Eleboo)
German Elektek
French Élektek
Italian Electabuzz
Spanish Electabuzz
Korean 에레브 (erebeu)
Chinese (Simplified) 电击兽
Chinese (Traditional) 電擊獸

 

English Electric Pokémon
Japanese でんげきポケモン
German Elektro-Pokémon
French Pokémon Électrique
Italian Pokémon Elettrico
Spanish Pokémon Eléctrico
Korean 전기포켓몬
Chinese (Simplified) 电击宝可梦
Chinese (Traditional) 電擊寶可夢

Name origin

electricity
energy from electric currents
buzz
low humming sound, as in electricity
